channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own, after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Residents flee border area as Afghan and Pakistani forces clash over crossing closure
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Residents fled a border area between Afghanistan and Pakistan after forces from both sides clashed into the early hours of Tuesday, a Pakistani official said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau slams Trump's tariffs, says US is appeasing Putin while launching a trade war against Canada
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has called U_S_ President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs “very dumb.”.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope stable with no new respiratory crises but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada may halt electricity exports to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Premier Doug Ford has threatened to cut off electricity exports to the US if President Trump's tariffs are enacted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Peru's Indigenous leaders raise concerns over oil and gas projects at a human rights hearing
item.description: Indigenous leaders from the Peruvian Amazon have urged the government to halt oil and gas projects that threaten their lands, presenting their case before the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights (IACHR).
item.dc:creator: Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pressured by Trump’s Gaza plan, Arab countries meet in Egypt to discuss alternative proposal
item.description: Arab nations met for a summit in Cairo, Egypt, on Tuesday with the goal of developing a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, aiming to rebuild without displacing residents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houthis designated as foreign terrorist organization, Rubio says
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Tuesday that the Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen are being designated as a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky calls Trump meeting 'regrettable,' stops short of apology
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday called his contentious meeting last week with President Trump at the White House a "regrettable" gathering but stopped short of issuing an apology for the public blowup.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump's halt on military aid will hurt Ukraine's defenses. But it may not be fatal
item.description: The U.S. has been Ukraine’s biggest military backer since Russia’s full-scale invasion began three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Serbian lawmakers throw smoke grenades, tear gas in parliament
item.description: Serbian opposition lawmakers let off smoke grenades and tear gas in parliament on Tuesday to protest against the government and support demonstrating students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indigenous migrants in northern Colombia battle worsening droughts and floods
item.description: MAICAO, Colombia (AP) — Standing next to her makeshift home of scrap metal, wood and plastic tarp, 47-year-old Nelly Mengual recounts how severe flooding and winds tore off her roof a few months ago, leaving her knee-deep in water in her home.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, The Hill

item.title: Department of Education clarifies school DEI guidelines, saying Black History Month doesn't violate order
item.description: The Department of Education clarified that celebrating Black History Month is not in violation of an order warning states to remove DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU ponders 800 billion euro plan to beef up defenses to counter possible US disengagement
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The chief of the European Union's executive on Tuesday proposed an 800 billion-euro ($841 billion) plan to beef up the defenses of EU nations, aiming to lessen the impact of potential U.S.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Timber over Tinder: German forest has connected lovers for over a century
item.description: Known as “Bräutigamseiche” in German, the Bridegroom’s Oak has a famous knothole that's been used as a mailbox since 1892.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators will question a driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in Germany, killing 2
item.description: Investigators will question the driver who allegedly rammed a car into a crowd in Germany's southwestern city of Mannheim, killing two people and wounding 11.
item.dc:creator: Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The economy and AI are likely to be among the top issues for China’s legislature
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Harnessing the power of artificial intelligence and shoring up the economy are among the issues expected to top the agenda when China’s legislature meets Wednesday in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Oval Office spat with Trump 'regrettable,' says he's ready to work for Ukraine peace
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says last week's Oval Office blowup with U_S_ counterpart Donald Trump was “regrettable,” and he adds: “It is time to make things right.”.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Greenlanders resisting Trump's talk of acquiring their homeland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenlanders have been pushed into the global spotlight in the weeks since U.S. President Donald Trump said America could take over their Arctic homeland. Most say they don’t want to be American. Many are worried and overwhelmed by the comments and the attention.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombian president aims to reduce drug violence and weaken rebels by paying farmers to switch crops
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro announced a plan Monday to reduce coca planting in a northeastern region rattled by rebel attacks by paying farmers while they switch to legal crops.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kim's sister threatens a response to a US carrier's deployment in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened Tuesday to launch high-profile provocations in response to the arrival of a U.S. aircraft carrier in South Korea and other U.S. military activities, which she slammed as “confrontation hysteria of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to expect when China's legislature meets this week
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s annual major political event gets underway this week, bringing together the nation’s top leaders and thousands of provincial leaders to endorse decisions already made by the all-powerful Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WANG FUJIYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers 2 acute respiratory crises, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two episodes of acute respiratory distress Monday as his prognosis remains "guarded" while at a hospital in Rome, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Car ramming in Mannheim, Germany, leaves 2 dead, multiple injured: reports
item.description: A car ramming incident left two people dead and multiple others injured in the city of Mannheim, Germany on Monday, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN rights chief laments 'shift in direction' in United States
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights chief has expressed concern about a “fundamental shift in direction” taking place in the United States when it comes to human rights. Volker Türk, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delegation of hostages released by Hamas traveling to DC to meet with Trump admin officials
item.description: A group of seven people who were freed from Hamas' captivity are traveling to Washington, D.C., to meet with officials from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel is criticized after it bars Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” for Hamas if a fragile ceasefire isn't extended.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
